transcript.text: Doing well, how's your day going?
Is Rick still planning on joining?
Oh, well, I wish we got a bit more viewers, but it is what it is.
So appreciate you guys taking the time.
This won't be the full 30 minutes.
I'm sure we can get through this in like 10 minutes or so.
But just looking to get a quick walkthrough of kind of how you guys stage and publish content to website today so our team can just make sure we don't miss any gotchas as we get into publishing.
Let me actually log into our WordPress and just kind of walk you through it really quick.
And then I told you I would share our step by step like blog process, which I never did.
So I'm going to do that now.
It's been a rough week back.
Do you guys have access to our Confluence by any chance?
Let me see if I can just download this and then share it with you via.
While I do that, let me log into WordPress.
Sorry, I don't know if you guys have met yet, but she is our content marketing coordinator.
She's on this call because she has pretty much taken over our blog scheduling.
So when we have internal employees who want to post a blog, she'll take care of that.
But just scheduling, organization, making sure that that blog goes out.
Great to meet you, Katherine, formally.
Now, let me share with you our blog process.
Oh, let me select you that, and then let me share.
Cool, so I'm sure you're familiar with WordPress.

Basically the process, and this is specifically just for blogs, but the blog process, how it goes is if I were to create a blog and go over to posts here, click add a post or add post, and it's going to take forever to load, as it always does.
It's to be one of those situations where you're trying to demo it and it won't work.
to tell us it helps us Right?
From here, it's pretty straightforward in terms of the content.
What we generally recommend, though, speaking from authors within SpecterOps, we have them just draft up their blog in a third-party tool, like WordPress, not WordPress, Google Docs, or WordPress.
What the hell am I thinking?
Not WordPress, but it's Microsoft Word.
And then from there, it's as easy as just kind of copying it over.
There's checkpoints here, though, that we go through when we're reviewing the blogs before posting is, one, the featured image, which is just clicking there and then uploading your file.

It takes like JPEG, PNG, it does not take web.p, it takes the normal stuff, but setting featured image, let's see, exit out of that, status, it's going to be in draft unless you schedule it or publish it, so just be aware of that.
If you schedule it, just typing in the time that you want it to go out, and then if you click publish, then it's going to pretty much be published.
So don't click publish until it's ready.
For the first couple, we'll probably save them in draft.
have you guys take a look at them and then have you publish, but then once we get into a comfortable cadence, we'll be getting published.
Once you have time to go through the step-by-step blog process, it basically...
Hopefully it tells you there to leave it in draft, and then give us a ping, and we'll take it from there.
And then moving down into categories, you're always going to have blog checkmarked, and then one of the other four categories.
So it'll be like two of them, or if it's none of them, just leave it as blog.
But blog will always be checked.
Next, if you scroll down here to the area here, you're going to want to include a meta description, just for SEO purposes.
Going down a bit more, you can select related posts to showcase on the blog page itself.
You don't have to, because it automatically does it, but if you want to manually do it, check manual, and then choose the post you want shown.
With these blog posts, will they include the TLDR?
So for our blog posts, we generally like to include a TLDR at the very top, one to two sentences max.
I'm not sure if that's the case for these, like, AI written ones.
If that's kind of your guys' preference, we can make sure those are included.
Okay, let's do that just to keep it the same.
Let me share this screen really quick.
Yeah, so like on our blog page, we include the TLDR so that when people are just glancing, they can get a quick look at what the blog's about.
I mean, I don't really have anything else.
Is there any fields that are required for it to be published, like author or date?
You don't really set a date in here.
It just kind of auto sets it when you publish.
One thing I will probably have to get you access to, if you don't already, is I have to give you permissions for author, contributor, and editor, I guess.
I don't know if you guys already have been given that, but you can check.
Let me give you that access.
And then is there a, um, like, SpecterOps team author or is there specific authors you guys want us to use when publishing?
We should have a, sorry, let me just finish these permissions really quick.
We don't, but let me see if I can get one created that's just SpecterOps so that it's just coming from our general brand.
Sorry, let me share my screen really quick just to...
Yeah, so it would be here.
We'll make it SpecterOps, but it does not show up here.
Do you guys use that focus key phrase down there for SEO?
Or is it just the meta description slug?
Yeah, just the meta description slug.
Everything else you can pretty much just ignore.
Well, if that's it, that's it.
Yeah, I mean, if you have any other questions or run into any issues, just give me a Slack and I should be able to figure it out for you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review SpecterOps' WordPress content publishing workflow.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=67.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Initial Workflow:** GrowthX will draft posts in WordPress and save them as drafts for SpecterOps' review before publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=339.0)
  - [**Mandatory Elements:** All posts require a featured image (no `.webp`), a meta description, and a 1–2 sentence TLDR at the top.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=286.0)
  - [**Author:** Ric will create a generic "SpecterOps" author profile to maintain brand consistency.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)
  - [**Permissions:** Ric granted GrowthX (`pod-0@growthx.ai`) `Contributor` access, enabling them to create and save drafts.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=483.0)

## Topics

### WordPress Publishing Workflow

  - [**Drafting:** Authors draft content in external tools (e.g., Google Docs) and paste it into WordPress.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=249.0)
  - [**Status Management:**](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=301.0)
      - [**Draft:** Default status for review; do not publish directly.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=301.0)
      - [**Schedule:** Set a future publish date/time.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=301.0)
      - [**Publish:** Makes the post live immediately.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=301.0)
  - [**Required Fields:**](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=286.0)
      - [**Featured Image:** Upload a JPEG or PNG file.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=286.0)
      - [**Categories:** Select "Blog" plus one other relevant category. If none fit, use "Blog" only.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=366.0)
      - [**Meta Description:** Write a short, SEO-friendly description.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=385.0)
  - [**Optional Fields:**](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=400.0)
      - [**Related Posts:** Manually select posts to display, overriding the default automatic selection.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=400.0)
      - [**SEO Tools:** Ignore the "Focus keyphrase" field; the meta description and slug are sufficient.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=656.0)

### Content Requirements

  - [**TLDR:** Include a 1–2 sentence "Too Long; Didn't Read" summary at the top of every post.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=426.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Improves user experience by providing a quick overview for skimmers, matching SpecterOps' existing blog format.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=451.0)

### Permissions & Author Profile

  - [**GrowthX Permissions:** Ric granted `pod-0@growthx.ai` `Contributor` access, which allows creating and saving drafts.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=483.0)
  - [**Author Profile:** Ric will create a generic "SpecterOps" author profile.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Ensures brand consistency by avoiding attribution to individual GrowthX authors.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Ric:** Create the "SpecterOps" author profile in WordPress.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)
  - [**GrowthX:** Review the Confluence blog process document (shared via Slack).](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=105.0)
  - [**GrowthX:** Draft initial posts in WordPress and save as drafts for SpecterOps' review.](https://fathom.video/share/qp1kBGizgtWaU1K5hwhnd1CNg2HUjL4h?tab=summary&timestamp=339.0)

action_items.description: Add TLDR (1–2 sentences) to all SpecterOps blog drafts
Create 'SpecterOps' WordPress author; then GrowthX set author on drafts
